landing.eyebrow: The harness fabric for AI coding agents
landing.tagline: Forge once. Run in every agent.
landing.subhead: Forge Development Hub centralizes, governs, and distributes skills, rules, agents, and hooks for Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, OpenAI Codex, and OpenCode.
landing.agentsHeading: Works with the agents you already use
landing.agentsSub: Install once — your components are materialized into every detected agent.
landing.primitivesSub: Everything Forge distributes is one of four composable kinds.
landing.primSkillBody: On-demand workflow guidance — procedures and references your agent loads when it needs them.
landing.primRuleBody: Always-on guidelines scoped by glob. One rule, one concern, applied automatically.
landing.primAgentBody: Specialized subagents — a system prompt, tools, and template packaged to drop in.
landing.primHookBody: Event-triggered commands (SessionStart, PreToolUse, …) wired into your agent config.
landing.howHeading: From zero to installed in three steps
landing.howSub: One CLI, every agent. No per-tool copy-paste.
landing.howStep1Body: Grab fdh via npm or a signed binary — macOS, Linux, or Windows.
landing.howStep2Body: Pick a harness; fdh resolves the components and detects your agents.
landing.howStep3Body: Your skills, rules, agents, and hooks appear in every detected tool.
landing.catalogSub: Real components, published and scanned. Browse the full catalog anytime.
landing.catalogEmpty: The catalog is warming up — explore it directly.
landing.trustSub: Open to read, controlled to publish.
landing.trustScanBody: Every published version carries a scan status, surfaced in the catalog.
landing.trustSignBody: Releases are tagged and shipped as signed bundles you can verify.
landing.trustSsoBody: Forge identity via Keycloak — roles, audit, and per-team approvals.
landing.harnessBody: A harness is a curated bundle of components. Reference one line in your manifest and your whole team gets the same setup — extend or trim per project.
landing.finalCtaBody: Install the CLI and have your first component running in minutes.
landing.feature1Body: One registry, four agents. Install skills, rules, agents, and hooks once and they appear in every tool you use.
landing.feature2Body: Forge identity via Keycloak. Roles, audit, and per-team approvals.
landing.feature3Body: Skills are 100% portable, following the open agentskills.io standard.

install.title: Install the fdh CLI
install.intro: Download the binary for your platform, verify the checksum, and place it on your PATH.
install.verifyHint: Before extracting, run the verification command to confirm the archive's integrity.

browse.title: Skill catalog
browse.searchPlaceholder: Search skills by name, namespace, or tag

catalog.title: {kind} catalog
catalog.searchPlaceholder: Search {kind} by name, namespace, or tag

componentDetail.installUnavailable: Direct `fdh install` currently supports skills. {kind} are materialized into your project via `fdh init` and hub harnesses.

admin.title: Admin
admin.intro: Operations console for the portal. Reads are derived from the served catalog and the hub Git history; privileged calls run server-side with a service credential.
admin.gatedMessage: Your account does not have the {group} group.
admin.statsDescription: Derived from the served catalog ({components}). No separate statistics store.
admin.healthDescription: Every catalog component with its real scan status. Built from the served catalog — no per-row re-scans.
admin.lifecycleProvisional: Lifecycle status is not yet tracked; every component reads active provisionally.
admin.activationDescription: Onboarding wizard events from the in-memory ring buffer.
admin.activationEmpty: No activation events recorded. The ring buffer is empty — it is cleared on every API restart.
admin.refreshDescription: Force an immediate refresh against the Git registry. Gated server-side at publisher minimum (admins satisfy it).
admin.errorPanelBody: Could not reach the admin API with the service credential. The catalog views above are unaffected. Detail: {detail}

footer.blurb: One registry for skills, rules, agents, and hooks — forged once, materialized into Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, OpenAI Codex, and OpenCode.

docs.title: Guides & docs
docs.intro: Everything you need to build a skill, rule, agent, or hook — and contribute it to the hub. The deep references live in the repo; this page is the map.
docs.primitivesLead: Pick the kind that fits what you're capturing. Each is a directory with one entry file.
docs.primSkillBody: On-demand workflow guidance — a procedure or reference that loads only when a task matches it.
docs.primRuleBody: An always-on guideline scoped by glob. One rule, one concern, applied automatically.
docs.primAgentBody: A specialized sub-agent: a system prompt plus a constrained tool allowlist.
docs.primHookBody: An event-triggered command (SessionStart, PreToolUse, …) wired into the agent host.
docs.installLead: Install the CLI once, then pull components into your project — from the Forge hub or your own source.
docs.installHubBody: fdh init walks you through picking a harness and materializes it into every agent you use. Already set up? fdh install applies your project's manifest.
docs.installExtBody: Point fdh at any registry you control — a Git repo, a local clone (air-gapped), or a static HTTP registry, with bearer / basic / mTLS auth. The default is the Forge hub.
docs.installExtNote: Local clone: set registry.local_path. HTTP wire protocol: set registry.kind: http. Every transport + auth option is in the CLI quickstart.
docs.createLead: Two paths produce the same result, byte for byte — CI runs the same checks either way. New components always start at version 0.1.0.
docs.cliHeading: Path A — with the fdh CLI (recommended)
docs.cliLead: Scaffold, materialize into every agent you use, then open the contribution PR — one flow.
docs.cliStep1Body: Writes a canonical, agent-agnostic source bundle (SKILL.md at 0.1.0) and materializes it into each agent you pick, so it works immediately. Use rule / agent / hook for the other kinds.
docs.cliStep2Body: Edit the canonical source, then regenerate every selected agent's copy. Use --check to report drift without overwriting.
docs.cliStep3Body: Validates the bundle (layout, frontmatter, portability lint, security scan), copies it into the hub, adds a registry entry with default: false, and opens the PR via gh. It never merges. No upstream write? It opens from your fork.
docs.noCliHeading: Path B — without the CLI (fork + edit + PR)
docs.noCliLead: No Go or fdh needed. Anything the CLI automates you can do by hand; CI runs the same checks.
docs.noCliStep1: Create the source directory + entry file from the per-kind template (start at version 0.1.0; hooks add hook.json).
docs.noCliStep2: Add the hub/registry.yaml entry: matching kind + path, every required field, and default: false.
docs.noCliStep3: Validate locally, commit with a scoped conventional message, then open the PR to the hub's main.
docs.validateLead: Run the exact check CI runs, from the repo root. It exits 0 with a one-line summary, or lists every problem (missing field, bad SemVer, kind/path mismatch, orphan directory).
docs.gatesHeading: Three gates — a PR doesn't auto-publish
docs.gatesLead: Opening a PR doesn't make your component part of the hub. It passes through three gates, each owned by a different role.
docs.gate1Body: No merge without green CI and a non-author CODEOWNERS approval. As the author, your job ends here: PR open, CI green, review requested.
docs.gate2Body: A version ships only when a publisher merges the release PR → tag → signed bundle. Merging your PR alone does not publish.
docs.gate3Body: A merged, published component stays default: false until an admin enables it or adds it to a harness. It can stay opt-in forever.
docs.referencesLead: The canonical, copy-pasteable guides live in the repo and are kept in sync with CI.
docs.refHubGuideDesc: What the hub is, the four primitives in depth, and when to use which.
docs.refAuthoringGuideDesc: Per-kind frontmatter templates, the registry entry, validation, and both contribution paths.
docs.refRunbookDesc: For admins: marking defaults, deprecation, the release pipeline, CODEOWNERS, scan_status.
docs.refContributingDesc: The gates and permission model, plus the team / namespace seed map — the canonical source.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.intro: Forge Development Hub is built to be usable by everyone, regardless of how they browse.
accessibility.commitmentBody: We target the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA across the portal. Accessibility is treated as a requirement, not an afterthought.
accessibility.feat1: Semantic, keyboard-navigable markup with visible focus states throughout.
accessibility.feat2: Color choices and a light/dark theme that aim to meet AA contrast ratios.
accessibility.feat3: Text alternatives for meaningful icons and imagery; decorative art is hidden from assistive tech.
accessibility.feat4: Responsive layouts that reflow without loss of content down to small viewports.
accessibility.feedbackBody: Accessibility is ongoing work. If something is hard to use with a keyboard, screen reader, or any assistive technology, please tell us — we treat it as a bug.

profile.title: Profile
profile.name: Name
profile.roleHint: Resolved from your Keycloak groups via the role-map precedence ladder. The portal API enforces roles independently on every request.
profile.groupsDescription: From your Keycloak token. The portal maps these to roles via the role-map config.
profile.contributionsDescription: Catalog components you authored, derived from forge-development-hub Git commit authorship matched by email — an email-match heuristic, not a verified identity link.
profile.contributionsEmptyHint: This matches your Keycloak email against Git commit authors. If your commit email differs, nothing will match — this is expected, not an error.
profile.contributionsError: Contributions are temporarily unavailable. The catalog and the rest of your profile are unaffected.
